steps.title: Compute the weighted sum for data set A
steps.content:
From the table, data set A has the following frequencies:
  • 0 appears 2 times
  • 1 appears 3 times
  • 2 appears 4 times
  • 3 appears 5 times
  • 4 appears 4 times
  • 5 appears 3 times
  • 6 appears 2 times
  • 7 appears 2 times
  • 8 appears 1 time
  • 9 appears 1 time
The weighted sum is $$\text{Sum}_A = 0\cdot2 + 1\cdot3 + 2\cdot4 + 3\cdot5 + 4\cdot4 + 5\cdot3 + 6\cdot2 + 7\cdot2 + 8\cdot1 + 9\cdot1 = 100$$.

steps.title: Find the mean of data set A
steps.content: Since there are 27 values, the mean of A is $$\mu_A = \frac{100}{27} \approx 3.70$$.

steps.title: Compute the weighted sum and mean for data set B
steps.content:
From the table, data set B has the following frequencies:
  • 0 appears 4 times
  • 1 appears 4 times
  • 2 appears 5 times
  • 3 appears 4 times
  • 4 appears 3 times
  • 5 appears 3 times
  • 6 appears 2 times
  • 7 appears 1 time
  • 8 appears 1 time
The weighted sum is $$\text{Sum}_B = 0\cdot4 + 1\cdot4 + 2\cdot5 + 3\cdot4 + 4\cdot3 + 5\cdot3 + 6\cdot2 + 7\cdot1 + 8\cdot1 = 80$$.
Thus the mean of B is $$\mu_B = \frac{80}{27} \approx 2.96$$.

steps.title: Compare the means and select the correct choice
steps.content:
Since $$\frac{100}{27} > \frac{80}{27}$$, we have $$\mu_A > \mu_B$$.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
